subject: [CCSDK-SDNC] Bump versions for Honolulu
commit_message:
[CCSDK-SDNC] Bump versions for Honolulu

Bump versions of CCSDK (including CDS) and SDNC images for
SDNC Honolulu release.

Fixed issues found in CDS command-executor and py-executor
pods.

Added missing env variable settings.


Issue-ID: CCSDK-3125
Issue-ID: SDNC-1473
Issue-ID: CCSDK-3192
Issue-ID: CCSDK-3197
Signed-off-by: Dan Timoney <dtimoney@att.com>
Change-Id: Id3bbe696313c568dc9ffb328715a7fc572330411

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 21 on the queue.

Patch Set 1:

In the process of creating release builds for CCSDK and SDNC ... marking this review as "work in
progress" until versions are ready.
Set Work In Progress

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1
> 
> APP_PORT environment variable is not set, using default.
> BASIC_AUTH environment variable is not set, using default.
> Traceback (most recent call last):
>   File "server.py", line 28, in 
>     import proto.CommandExecutor_pb2_grpc as CommandExecutor_pb2_grpc
>   File "/opt/app/onap/python/proto/CommandExecutor_pb2_grpc.py", line 4, in 
>     import CommandExecutor_pb2 as CommandExecutor__pb2
> ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'CommandExecutor_pb2'

The unset variable statements are just warnings (not related to the ModuleNotFoundError).
The ModuleNotFoundError is due to a typo on the import.  In Java, the compiler would have
caught that, but no such luck with Python unfortunately.  We'll need to re-release CDS
dockers with the fix.  Should be ready tomorrow (I already pushed the fix - now it's just
a matter of getting it reviewed/merged, rebuilding and re-releasing dockers).

Right - thanks for redeploying.  Maybe we'll be luckier this time.  There are 2 errors that I do see in the log that are unrelated (we've seen both before), but which are flooding the logs:

1) The A1 policy management API does not seem to be configured with a RIC ip address.  So, it keeps sending a message to the controller that is missing an IP, and so we keep seeing that error repeat.  I'll open a Jira for that and get someone from the ORAN team to work it.

2) Some process keeps SDNC a message with bad credentials.  These messages repeat about every 15 seconds - so my guess is this is a misconfigured healthcheck somewhere.  Unfortunately, OpenDaylight doesn't log the source IP or even the target URL that is failing, so there's not much to go on in the log to isolate this error.  I haven't seen this in my local env, where I run minimal ONAP (A&AI, AAF,DMAAP message router, ASDC, SO) - so we can probably rule those out.

FYI ... I finally found the culprit for the repeated authentication failures in the SDNC log (about every 15 seconds) ... consul is sending a jolokia query to SDNC with the wrong password.  I'll push a  change to fix that, but really that consul/values.yaml should really be using a secret for that password (it's hard coded now as "admin").
Hashtag added: beforeM3

Patch Set 12: Code-Review-1

all the E2E tests involving the full chain SDC - SO - SDNC -AAI seem to be fail due to tiemout on SO requests (A la carte BPMN)
there is no error reported by SO (each request in is IN  PROGRESS during 15 minutes)
however if we look into the pod logs from the cluster snapshot executed after the tests (k8s-teardown), we can see exception in SDNC

see https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//118284-12/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s-teardown/kubernetes-status/pod-onap-sdnc-0-sdnc-logs.html

Patch Set 12: Code-Review-1

network name gen fails on the last tests

Patch Set 12:

> Patch Set 12: Code-Review-1
> 
> all the E2E tests involving the full chain SDC - SO - SDNC -AAI seem to be fail due to tiemout on SO requests (A la carte BPMN)
> there is no error reported by SO (each request in is IN  PROGRESS during 15 minutes)
> however if we look into the pod logs from the cluster snapshot executed after the tests (k8s-teardown), we can see exception in SDNC
> 
> see https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//118284-12/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s-teardown/kubernetes-status/pod-onap-sdnc-0-sdnc-logs.html

The exception is unrelated to SO:
org.onap.ccsdk.sli.core.sli.SvcLogicException: Exception while posting http request to client URI is not absolute
	at org.onap.ccsdk.sli.plugins.restapicall.RestapiCallNode.sendHttpRequest(RestapiCallNode.java:855) ~[bundleFile:?]
	at org.onap.ccsdk.sli.plugins.restapicall.RestapiCallNode.sendRequest(RestapiCallNode.java:501) [bundleFile:?]
	at org.onap.ccsdk.sli.plugins.restapicall.RestapiCallNode.sendRequest(RestapiCallNode.java:474) [bundleFile:?]
	at jdk.internal.reflect.GeneratedMethodAccessor105.invoke(Unknown Source) ~[?:?]
	at jdk.internal.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(Unknown Source) ~[?:?]

This is actually an issue in A1 policy management (see https://jira.onap.org/browse/CCSDK-3227).  By default, it has no non-RT RICs configured, and so should not be sending a message to SDNC.  However, it is sending an invalid message, which in turn causes this error.  This shouldn't hurt anything, but we will address it so that it doesn't flood the logs and obscure true errors.

Patch Set 12:

> Patch Set 12: Code-Review-1
> 
> network name gen fails on the last tests

I see the problem.  We changed to use the integration image, and forgot that the integration image defines an ENTRYPOINT that assumes the code to be executed is in /app.jar - which is not the case here.  Simple fix - I'll push an update

Patch Set 12:

> Patch Set 12:

> 
> I see the problem.  We changed to use the integration image, and forgot that the integration image defines an ENTRYPOINT that assumes the code to be executed is in /app.jar - which is not the case here.  Simple fix - I'll push an update

Correction - the jar actually is in /app.jar, but for whatever reason, the startup script first does a 'touch' on that file - probably intended to make sure it exists.  Unfortunately, /app.jar is owned by root, so touch fails.  So, I'll need to update the image. I'll create a new image and update this review with the new number

> Patch Set 29:
> 
> PS28 still doesn't work: https://gitlab.com/Orange-OpenSource/lfn/onap/xtesting-onap/-/pipelines/278438561
> You should get full results posted shortly but I already see that basic_cnf, basic_vnf and pnf_registrate were failed

For basic_cnf, it looks like some sort of timing issue in SO - they are rejecting our asynchronous response with an error indicating they are not expecting a response for that transaction.

For basic_vm, we are getting a 401 Unauthorized response from SO. This is the same error we got before my patch to set the SO credential variables. However, it doesn’t make sense that we’d get a 401 in this case but not for basic_cnf.

Let’s see if we have better luck with my last change set.

just to precise that for the #41, pnf-registrate worked but not the first time
pnf-registrate is launched as a job
in case of Error this job is retried
if it is green on the dashboard it means that it was PASS
but the artifact is the one of the first attempt that was FAIL

second try result PASS reported at 2021-03-31 13:48:00
in theory it shall work the first time...but sometimes it remains stuck in SO.
